# UC-13.2.7 · Predictive Service Degradation

## Description

Predicting service health degradation before it happens enables proactive remediation, reducing incident impact.

## Value

Predicting service health degradation before it happens enables proactive remediation, reducing incident impact.

## Implementation

Train ML models on service health history using MLTK. Predict health scores 4-24 hours ahead. Alert when predicted health falls below threshold. Investigate contributing KPIs proactively. This is an advanced ITSI capability.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI + MLTK.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `itsi_summary` + ML models.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Train ML models on service health history using MLTK. Predict health scores 4-24 hours ahead. Alert when predicted health falls below threshold. Investigate contributing KPIs proactively. This is an advanced ITSI capability.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsi_summary service_name="Production Web"
| timechart span=15m avg(health_score) as health
| predict health as predicted_health future_timespan=24 algorithm=LLP5
| where predicted_health < 50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Predictive Service Degradation** — Predicting service health degradation before it happens enables proactive remediation, reducing incident impact.

Documented **Data sources**: `itsi_summary` + ML models. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI + MLTK. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsi_summary.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsi_summary. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Pipeline stage (see **Predictive Service Degradation**): predict health as predicted_health future_timespan=24 algorithm=LLP5
• Filters the current rows with `where predicted_health < 50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when you know the service or episode context. Compare with the ITSI Service Analyzer, episode or notable views, and Glass Tables or drill-downs for the same time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (actual vs predicted health), Single value (predicted health in 4h), Alert timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=itsi_summary service_name="Production Web"
| timechart span=15m avg(health_score) as health
| predict health as predicted_health future_timespan=24 algorithm=LLP5
| where predicted_health < 50
```

## Visualization

Line chart (actual vs predicted health), Single value (predicted health in 4h), Alert timeline.
